title: NOC 10010 – Financial Managers | GTR Immigration
description: Financial Managers classified under NOC 10010 fall within one of the most senior and in-demand occupational groups in Canada’s immigration system. These professionals play a vital leadership role in shaping corporate strategy, managing financial risk, and ensuring long-term organizational growth—making them highly attractive candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).
duties: Plan and evaluate accounting, audit, and financial departments
Design and implement financial systems and policies
Prepare financial statements, cost-benefit studies, and executive reports
Assess investment strategies and reporting systems
Recruit, train, and manage finance staff
Communicate with shareholders and financial analysts
Guide mergers, acquisitions, and capital investments
Report major financial trends and risks to senior executives
employment_requirements: Occupations in TEER 0 usually require a university degree or college diploma and several years of experience in a related field.
job_outlook: Over the next decade, Canada is projected to create 32,100 new positions for Financial Managers, while around 36,900 job seekers are expected to enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—particularly those with senior-level experience—this presents strong opportunities when supported by a well-planned immigration strategy.
labour_market_demand: Financial Managers are experiencing sustained national demand, driven by post-pandemic economic growth, business expansion, and increasingly complex regulatory environments. Organizations across banking, corporate finance, insurance, and government sectors continue to seek experienced financial leaders.
Strong hiring prospects across provinces and territories
Excellent immigration prospects for qualified overseas professionals
Annual earnings commonly range from CAD $57,000 to over $315,000, depending on experience, location, and specialization.
Professionals immigrating under NOC 10010 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
A clear pathway to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
A diverse and welcoming multicultural society

Financial Managers are senior professionals who plan, direct, and oversee an organization’s financial operations. Their work supports both day-to-day business performance and long-term strategic planning.
Advising senior leadership on business decisions
These roles exist across sectors such as banking, corporate services, insurance, public administration, and large multinational firms.
benefits: In addition to salaries, many employers provide:
Dental, medical, and life insurance coverage
Paid maternity, parental, and caregiver leave
Employer contributions to national pension and employment insurance programs
pathways: At GTR Immigration, we specialize in helping senior professionals navigate Canada’s complex immigration system. Our consultants assess your profile, CRS score, and provincial options to design a tailored pathway toward Permanent Residence under Express Entry or PNP streams.
Thinking of immigrating to Canada as a Financial Manager (NOC 10010)? Connect with GTR Immigration today for a personalized eligibility assessment and strategic immigration roadmap.
